title: 'How the mind works' — Mar 2019 title-only seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Mar 19, 2019, 10:33pm — Yahir is 16, junior year at Grants Pass High. Title is the entire artifact: "How the mind works, how it thinks of things, shit in that realm." Body is empty. 25-second create→modify gap.
  • Pure — Yahir captures the topic he intended to write about but the actual writing never happens. The title is a placeholder for an essay that never got drafted.
  • The phrasing — "shit in that realm" — is vintage teen-Yahir, mixing the philosophical ambition (cognition, metacognition) with the casual-vernacular hedge. The same voice that produces the Sept 2019 "money means nothing" aphorism six months later.
  • Sits in the broader 2018-2019 metacognition cluster with "Impact evolution" (also title-only) and the later coding-zeal / simplicity-manifesto thread — Yahir is repeatedly opening the file labeled "how does my own thinking work" and then closing it without writing.
  • The note is the earliest corpus appearance of the metacognition-as-recurring-itch pattern that later matures into asking the right question and note as letter to self frames.

Source

"How the mind works, how it thinks of things, shit in that realm"

Why it matters in the graph

  • A canonical example of the title-as-essay-prompt-never-written sub-pattern of the apple-notes junk-drawer form — the note that proves the idea was had even if the work was never done.
  • Together with 2018 10 09 impact evolution and the empty-body 2020-09-05 "why and where transmute" note, this forms a triad of teen-era essay-titles-without-essays.

Open questions

  • Did the topic ever get its essay? No follow-up note found in 2019.

Wikilinks

asking the right question · grants pass or · 2018 10 09 impact evolution · 2020 09 05 why and where are you going to transmute · 2019 09 17 money means nothing do what you love and want to do

related.label: 'Why and where are you going to transmute' — Sept 2020 title-only seed
